$schema_note: Cross-stack PR-body equivalence fixture. The TS test in packages/sdk-ts/tests/pr-body-cross-stack.test.ts AND the Python test in python/gravel/tests/test_pr_body_cross_stack.py both load this file, feed `input` into their composeBody / compose_pr_body implementation, and assert the rendered string is byte-equal to `expectedBody`. Olly's de_platform PR #249 (2026-05-20) shipped without the manifest explainer + feedback link because the Python stack had a stub _compose_body that pre-dated v0.8.0 — these fixtures are now the cross-stack canary.

cases.name: first_add — manifest didn't exist, single added file
cases.expectedBody:
On behalf of Olly.

**About `.gravel/manifest.json`:** this PR also adds the Gravel manifest. It tracks which prompts in this repo are managed by the embedded dashboard — your team edits these files in-app and Gravel opens a PR like this one when they hit Submit. Keep the file in the repo; future updates need it to know what lives where.

---
<sub>PR created via [Gravel](https://gravel.artanis.ai). [Send feedback →](https://gravel.artanis.ai/feedback?repo=artanis-ai%2Fde_platform&branch=gravel%2Fdraft-2026-05-20-olly)</sub>

cases.name: all six diff kinds in a single submit
cases.expectedBody:
**Manifest changes** (`.gravel/manifest.json`):
- Added prompt `prompts/a.md` (id `p_a`). New entry tracked by the manifest.
- Updated prompt at `prompts/b.md` (id `p_b`). The content hash changed — that's the actual edit you're reviewing.
- Removed prompt `prompts/c.md` (id `p_c`). The manifest no longer tracks this file.
- Renamed: `old/d.md` → `prompts/d.md` (id `p_d`). Same content (same hash); the manifest follows the move.
- Updated inline-prompt anchors for `src/agent.py` (id `p_e`). The surrounding code shifted; the start/end markers moved with it.

---
